formations_blocks: England trying to play out from the back
Croatia starting strongly with an aggressive press/high-pressure start
Croatia defending the initial corner and threatening from set pieces
adjustments: Kane drops deep to link play
Gordon makes runs down the left looking to be released by Rice
Madueke provides a threat down the right after a loose touch from Modric
key_matchups: Rice vs Perisic (Rice intercepts Perisic's low cross)
Stones vs Croatia's early press (England's buildup under pressure)
Modric vs Madueke (Modric's error leads to the penalty)
Kane vs Livakovic (saved first penalty, then scored the retake)
phases.summary: Croatia began the phase on the front foot, pressing England’s build-up and creating early danger from corners and wide deliveries. England were initially shaky, but a turnover from Modric and Madueke’s reaction won a penalty, which Kane eventually converted on the retake after Gvardiol encroached.

formations_blocks: Croatia pressing/high stepping to attack and recover possession
adjustments: Kane dropping deep to link play
Madueke stretching the right side and attacking in behind
Bellingham driving from the left channel
England becoming more dangerous on transitions after initially being second best
key_matchups: James vs Musa
Gvardiol vs Kane
Livakovic vs Kane
phases.summary: The phase opens with Croatia pushing forward and England surviving a dangerous right-sided cross, but England begin to look increasingly threatening once they can break quickly. Kane drops deeper to connect play, Madueke provides width on the right, and Bellingham carries the ball from the left, with the rhythm briefly interrupted by the hydration break and the retake drama. Croatia level through Baturina, but England respond quickly through Kane to restore control in a transition-heavy, end-to-end spell.

adjustments: England described as having 'fallen back into fearful patterns' and being 'confused' and 'complicated'
England appeared to receive a firm half-time talking-to
Second half restarted by Croatia after England were late back on the pitch
key_matchups: Declan Rice’s corners vs Croatia’s set-piece preparation
O’Reilly unmarked at the far post from Rice’s corner
phases.summary: The half-time message from England’s staff suggests a side that had drifted into nervous, overcomplicated habits despite leading the game’s chance creation. The key tactical note in the brief is England’s continued threat from Rice’s corners, with one delivery immediately producing a far-post header chance for O’Reilly, underlining Croatia’s vulnerability at set pieces.

formations_blocks: Croatia pass and probe, suggesting a settled possession shape rather than an explicit high press or low block
England attacking through sustained pressure and repeated corners after the restart
adjustments: Croatia tried to settle after conceding by circulating possession and using Modric’s free-kick delivery
England increasingly funnelled attacks down the right via James and Madueke, then kept pressure on through set pieces
Rice also began driving from the left flank, adding a different attacking route
key_matchups: Bellingham vs Pasalic
James vs Modric on the free kick
Rice vs Livakovic
Madueke vs Modric
O’Reilly vs Livakovic
Gordon vs Livakovic
phases.summary: The phase is defined by a swing in momentum immediately after the interval: England score again at 47' and then pin Croatia back with wave after wave of pressure, especially from set pieces and right-sided combinations. Croatia respond by trying to steady possession through Modric and Baturina, but they are largely on the back foot, surviving thanks to Livakovic’s saves and clearances under heavy pressure.

formations_blocks: England protecting a lead and looking to shore things up
Croatia pushing on for a response in the closing stages
adjustments: Croatia double substitution: Kramaric and Vlasic on for Mario Pasalic and Baturina
England substitution: Spence on for Bellingham, suggesting a more defensive shape to hold the lead
key_matchups: Rashford vs Stanisic on England's left
Marco Pasalic vs Pickford from a tight angle
Matanovic vs Pickford from distance
Kramaric vs Pickford after crossing from the left
Spence vs Livakovic in a late England break
Kovacic vs Pickford from range
phases.summary: This phase is open and tense, with Croatia committing bodies forward in search of a way back while England sit on their lead and then add another late goal. Croatia create a series of chances from the right, the left, and from distance, but Pickford and England’s last line hold firm, while Livakovic is also tested at the other end. England’s substitution of Spence for Bellingham points to game management, but Rashford’s 85th-minute goal effectively settles the contest.
